input.prompt:
Explore this codebase thoroughly. I need to understand:
1. What is this project? (read README, package.json, any design docs)
2. What's the overall architecture and directory structure?
3. What are the main features/modules?
4. Look for TODO comments, FIXME, NotImplemented, "not yet", "stub", "placeholder", "coming soon", or similar markers of incomplete work
5. Look for empty function bodies, pass statements, or functions that just raise/throw
6. Check for any design docs, specs, or issue trackers that describe planned features

Be very thorough - check all directories, config files, and source files. Report back with a comprehensive summary.

1 # Comic Studio 2 3 **An experiment in collaborative AI workflows for comic book creation.** 4 5 Comic Studio explores what happens when AI isn't a tool you operate but a peer you co-create with. There are no "generate" buttons wired to rigid templates. Instead, every interaction happens through natural conversation with context-aware AI agents that understand where you are in the creative process and what tools are relevant. 6 7 The AI is another voice in the room — not a sycophant that agrees with everything, not a steamroller that takes over. It's a collaborator that can brainstorm story ideas, draft dialogue, generate character art, and push back on creative choices when something isn't working. The creator stays in control, but the AI has a real seat at the table. 8 9

10 11 12

13

Fully AI-generated comic covers, created through conversation — not forms.

14 15 --- 16 17 ## Why This Exists 18 19 Most creative tools treat AI as a feature bolted onto a traditional interface: click a button, get an output, copy-paste it somewhere. The creator tosses a request over the wall and hopes something useful comes back. That's not collaboration — that's a vending machine. 20 21 Comic Studio is an experiment in a different model. The AI is a co-creator with its own perspective: 22 23 - **No wired buttons.** Creation, editing, and rendering all flow through natural language. There's no "Generate" button — you talk to the AI the way you'd talk to a collaborator sitting next to you. 24 - **Co-creation, not handoff.** The AI doesn't generate assets for you to drag into a separate editor. You work together in a shared workspace where story structure, character data, style references, and generated art all live side by side. 25 - **A peer, not a servant.** The AI can push back, suggest alternatives, and challenge choices that aren't working. No sycophancy, no steamrolling — just another voice in the room with useful skills. 26 - **Context-aware agents.** The system dynamically selects from 14 specialized agents based on what you're working on. Editing a panel? The agent already knows the scene, the characters in frame, the art style, and the dialogue. It doesn't ask you to re-explain. 27 28 --- 29 30 ## What It Produces 31 32 Comic Studio manages the full creative hierarchy — from series bible to individual panel — and generates imagery that maintains visual consistency across an entire issue. 33 34 ### Style System 35 36 Define an art style once and apply it everywhere. The system supports separate style definitions for art, characters, and dialogue elements (speech bubbles, thought bubbles, sound effects, narration boxes). Generate style examples as visual anchors before committing to a look. 37 38

39 40 41 42

43

The same scene prompt rendered across three different art styles.

44 45 ### Character Consistency 46 47 Characters are defined with structured appearance, attire, and behavior descriptions. Each character can have multiple variants (costumes, ages, disguises), and every variant can be rendered as a style reference sheet with turnarounds and expression studies. 48 49

50 51 52 53

54

The same character variant — "Brassic in gnome disguise" — rendered consistently across three art styles with turnaround poses and expression studies.

55 56 ### Dialog Styles 57 58 Each comic style includes distinct visual treatments for different dialog types — normal speech, whispers, shouts, thoughts, narration, and sound effects — generated as reference examples to lock in the visual language. 59 60

61 62 63 64

65

Dialog style examples: chat, thought, and shout bubbles in vintage four-color.

66 67 --- 68 69 ## Architecture 70 71 ### AI-First Design 72 73 The application is built on the **OpenAI Agents SDK** with function-calling tools, not a simple chat wrapper. Each of the 14 agent types has: 74 75 - A **custom persona** with system instructions tailored to its role (story editor, character designer, cover artist, etc.) 76 - A **filtered toolkit** — only the tools relevant to the current context are available, preventing hallucinated actions 77 - **Full object context** — the agent receives the current selection's data, parent hierarchy, and related assets as structured context 78 79 This means you can say *"make the dialogue punchier"* while viewing a panel, and the agent knows exactly which panel, which scene it belongs to, what characters are in frame, and what the art style is — without you specifying any of it. 80 81 ### 60+ Function Tools 82 83 Tools cover the full CRUD lifecycle plus specialized creative operations: 84 85 | Category | Examples | 86 |----------|----------| 87 | **Story** | Create/edit series, issues, scenes, panels, dialogue, narration | 88 | **Characters** | Create characters, define variants, manage appearance/attire/behavior | 89 | **Visual** | Generate panel images, covers, character reference sheets, style examples | 90 | **Editing** | Inpainting, outpainting, region-based image editing with conversational steering | 91 | **Style** | Define art/character/dialog styles, generate visual anchors | 92 | **Navigation** | Context-aware selection changes through the data hierarchy | 93 94 ### Studio Workspace UI 95 96 Built with **NiceGUI** (Python web framework), the interface follows a studio layout: 97 98 - **Breadcrumb navigation** through the creative hierarchy (Series > Issue > Scene > Panel) 99 - **Split view** — structured data editing on the left, AI conversation on the right 100 - **Image grids** with render, select, and delete for managing generated assets 101 - **Reference image uploads** to guide AI generation with visual context 102 - **Voice input/output** — dictate instructions and hear AI responses 103 - **Dark mode** with consistent theming 104 105

106 107

108

The studio workspace: structured editing on the left, AI conversation on the right.

109 110 --- 111 112 ## Data Model 113 114 ``` 115 Publisher (brand, logo) 116 └─ Series (premise, description) 117 ├─ Characters 118 │ └─ Variants (appearance, attire, behavior) 119 │ └─ Styled Reference Images 120 ├─ Issues (story, metadata, credits) 121 │ ├─ Scenes (narrative, setting, style) 122 │ │ └─ Panels (beat, description, dialogue, narration, aspect ratio, images) 123 │ └─ Covers (description, character refs, images) 124 └─ Comic Styles 125 ├─ Art Style (linework, inking, color palette) 126 ├─ Character Style (form, proportions) 127 └─ Dialog Styles (bubble types, typography, effects) 128 ``` 129 130 Every entity is a Pydantic model with full validation. The hierarchy is navigable in the UI and understood by the AI agents. 131 132 --- 133 134 ## Tech Stack 135 136 | Layer | Technology | 137 |-------|-----------| 138 | **Language** | Python 3.12+ | 139 | **UI Framework** | NiceGUI 2.19+ | 140 | **AI Orchestration** | OpenAI Agents SDK | 141 | **LLM** | GPT-5.2 (text generation, function calling) | 142 | **Image Generation** | gpt-image-1.5 (generation, inpainting, outpainting) | 143 | **Data Validation** | Pydantic 2.11+ | 144 | **Image Processing** | Pillow 11.2+ | 145 | **Storage** | Local JSON with abstract storage interface | 146 | **Logging** | Loguru | 147 148 --- 149 150 ## Creative Workflow 151 152 1. **Define your style** — Create a comic style with art, character, and dialog parameters. Generate visual examples to lock in the look. 153 2. **Build your cast** — Create characters with variants (costumes, ages, forms). Generate styled reference sheets for visual consistency. 154 3. **Write your story** — Create a series and issue. Collaborate with the AI to develop the story summary. 155 4. **Break it down** — Divide the issue into scenes with narrative summaries. The AI can suggest scene breaks from your story. 156 5. **Panel by panel** — Define each panel's beat, visual description, dialogue, and narration. The AI drafts from scene context. 157 6. **Generate art** — Render panel images and covers. The system builds prompts from structured data: character descriptions, scene context, style parameters, and reference images. 158 7. **Refine** — Use conversational image editing (inpainting/outpainting) to adjust generated art. Select finals from the image grid. 159 160 Every step is conversational. You're never filling out a form — you're talking to a collaborator who happens to have a structured understanding of your entire project. 161 162 --- 163 164 ## Getting Started 165 166 ```bash 167 # Clone the repository 168 git clone 169 cd comics 170 171 # Create a virtual environment 172 python3 -m venv .venv 173 source .venv/bin/activate 174 175 # Install dependencies 176 pip install -r requirements.txt 177 178 # Set your OpenAI API key 179 echo "OPENAI_API_KEY=your-key-here" > .env 180 181 # Run the application 182 python main.py 183 ``` 184 185 The app launches a local web server (default: http://localhost:8080). 186 187 --- 188 189 ## Key Design Decisions 190 191 - **Conversation over configuration.** Every creative action flows through the chat. This isn't laziness — it's a deliberate choice to keep the creator in a flow state rather than switching between forms and menus. 192 - **Agents over endpoints.** Instead of REST APIs behind buttons, the system uses function-calling agents that reason about which tools to invoke based on natural language input and current context. 193 - **Style as a first-class entity.** Styles aren't filters applied after the fact. They're structured definitions (art, character, dialog) that propagate through every generation call, ensuring visual coherence across an entire series. 194 - **Reference images as creative guardrails.** Uploaded references and generated style examples are fed into every image generation call, giving the AI visual context alongside textual descriptions. 195 196 --- 197 198 ## Project Status 199 200 This is an active experiment in AI-first application design and collaborative creative workflows. The core loop — from series creation through panel image generation — is functional and producing real output. It's not production software; it's a working exploration of what co-creation with AI can look like when the AI is treated as a peer rather than a tool. 201 202 --- 203 204 ## License 205 206 All rights reserved. 207 [result-id: r2]

1	# Bug List
2	
3	- `agentic/tools/reader.py:99` — `any(pk.keys(), ...)` is invalid and raises `TypeError`; replace with a generator check like `any(k not in TOP_LEVEL_IDS for k in pk)`.
4	- `agentic/tools/navigation.py:64-75` — `select_series` mutates `state.selection` directly, bypassing `change_selection`, so breadcrumbs/details don’t refresh and state isn’t persisted.
5	- `messaging.py:97-104` — `handle_message_output_event` ignores the generated message; no UI output is appended for tool-driven responses.
6	- `storage/local.py:335-347` — `find_reference_image` references undefined `relation` and uses the uploads list rather than reference uploads, causing lookup failures.
7	- `main.py:64` — missing space when concatenating `MIDDLE_STYLING_CLASSES` results in an invalid class string for the middle pane.
8	- `schema/reference_image.py:18-27` — `id` returns only `relation.value`, producing non-unique IDs and potential overwrites in storage.

1	# UX Ideas: Path-Based Views for Multi-Window Sessions
2	
3	## Problem
4	Today the app generates pages on the fly at the root path. Because the URL never changes, users cannot open multiple browser windows (or tabs) that point to different objects in the same session. We need stable, path-based routes so each window can deep-link to a specific view while sharing the same authenticated session.
5	
6	## Goals
7	- Each window has a distinct URL that encodes the current selection.
8	- URLs are deep-linkable and reload-safe.
9	- Session auth stays shared across windows (cookie-based).
10	- Back/forward works in a predictable way.
11	
12	## Design Alternatives
13	
14	### 1) Hierarchical Resource Routes (most direct)
15	Use explicit, human-readable paths for the current selection, with IDs embedded.
16	
17	Examples:
18	- `/series/:seriesId`
19	- `/series/:seriesId/issue/:issueId`
20	- `/series/:seriesId/issue/:issueId/scene/:sceneId`
21	- `/series/:seriesId/issue/:issueId/scene/:sceneId/panel/:panelId`
22	- `/styles/:styleId`, `/characters/:characterId`, `/publishers/:publisherId`
23	
24	Pros:
25	- Clear, shareable URLs and breadcrumbs.
26	- Works naturally with browser history.
27	- Easy to add “Open in new window” using the current URL.
28	
29	Cons:
30	- Requires consistent routing rules across all entities.
31	- Needs server fallback for client-side routes (serve app shell for all routes).
32	
33	### 2) Workspace + Selection Query (simple and flexible)
34	Keep a workspace path and encode the selection in the query string.
35	
36	Examples:
37	- `/workspace/:workspaceId?sel=series:123`
38	- `/workspace/:workspaceId?sel=issue:456&parent=series:123`
39	- `/workspace/:workspaceId?sel=panel:999&parent=scene:321`
40	
41	Pros:
42	- Minimal routing surface; the app always loads one route.
43	- Easy to extend without restructuring path hierarchy.
44	- Works well if selection types change frequently.
45	
46	Cons:
47	- URLs are less readable.
48	- Requires parsing to build breadcrumbs.
49	
50	### 3) View State IDs (server-stored view snapshots)
51	When a user navigates, create a short-lived view state on the server and use it in the URL.
52	
53	Examples:
54	- `/view/:viewId` where `viewId` maps to `{ selection, breadcrumbs, filters }`.
55	
56	Pros:
57	- Fully decouples UI state from URL structure.
58	- Can include complex UI state (filters, tabs, grid modes).
59	
60	Cons:
61	- Not transparent; URLs are opaque.
62	- Requires storage and TTL policies.
63	- Deep links expire unless persisted.
64	
65	### 4) Generic Resource Route + Tab Context
66	Use a consistent path shape for all resources and store the UI “panel” or mode as a query.
67	
68	Examples:
69	- `/r/series/:id?tab=issues`
70	- `/r/scene/:id?tab=panels`
71	- `/r/panel/:id?tab=images`
72	
73	Pros:
74	- Very small router surface area.
75	- Easy to add new entity types.
76	
77	Cons:
78	- Less semantic than hierarchical routes.
79	- Requires more client logic to resolve context.
80	
81	## Recommendation (if we want clarity + shareable links)
82	Start with **Alternative 1 (Hierarchical Resource Routes)** or **Alternative 4 (Generic Resource Route)**.
83	
84	- Alt 1 is best for readability and user mental model.
85	- Alt 4 is best for engineering speed and extensibility.
86	
87	Both approaches support multiple windows because each window has its own URL, and session auth can stay cookie-based. The critical change is to move selection state into the URL (path and/or query) instead of keeping it only in a single in-memory root view.
88	
89	## UX Notes
90	- Provide a visible “Open in new window” action near breadcrumbs or headers.
91	- Make breadcrumbs reflect the URL so sharing a link preserves context.
92	- On refresh, rehydrate selection from URL and load data accordingly.
93	
94	## Multi-Window Constraint: Single-Threaded Chat Context
95	Current chat context is single-threaded and global. This prevents parallel, scoped conversations per window, which is a core expectation for MDI (each window/tab should carry its own context and chat history).
96	
97	Implications:
98	- Opening two windows would mix or overwrite conversation context.
99	- Replies could target the wrong selection if both windows share one chat thread.
100	
101	Mitigation Ideas:
102	- Introduce `conversation_id` scoped to the URL (e.g., query param or route state).
103	- Bind chat history and AI context to `conversation_id` + selection path.
104	- Optionally allow “fork conversation” when opening a new window, so users can branch context intentionally.
105

1	"""
2	This module handles the messaging functionality for the application, including sending messages,
3	and updating the message history in the user interface.   The one complexity to this is that
4	If the agent needs to call a tool, then multiple internal messages will be generated.   These
5	are posed to a special "thoughts" container that can be expanded by the user to see the details.
6	"""
7	
8	import asyncio
9	from agents import Runner, ItemHelpers
10	from loguru import logger
11	from nicegui import ui
12	from openai.types.responses import ResponseTextDeltaEvent
13	from agents import AgentUpdatedStreamEvent, RunItemStreamEvent
14	from gui.state import APPState
15	
16	
17	
18	ROLE_MAP = {
19	    "you": "user",
20	    "bot": "assistant",
21	    "tool output": "assistant",
22	    "tool call": "assistant",
23	}
24	
25	def append_history(sender: str, content: ui.element, sent: bool = True):
26	    """
27	    Append a message to the chat history in the user interface.
28	    """
29	    with ui.chat_message(name=sender, sent=sent).classes('w-full') as message:
30	        ui.markdown(content)
31	    return message
32	
33	
34	def thoughts_container(parent: ui.row) -> ui.expansion:
35	    """
36	    If there is a expansion in the parent, return it, otherwise create a new one.
37	    """
38	    # if we’ve already created it, reuse it
39	    if hasattr(parent, "_thoughts_expand"):
40	        return parent._thoughts_expand
41	
42	    # otherwise create it and stash it on the parent
43	    with parent:
44	        parent._thoughts_expand = (
45	            ui.expansion("Thoughts", value=False)
46	              .classes("w-full text-sm")
47	        )
48	    return parent._thoughts_expand
49	
50	def create_spinner() -> ui.spinner:
51	    """
52	    Create a spinner element for indicating loading or processing.
53	    """
54	    return 
55	
56	
57	async def handle_text_delta_event(state: APPState,event: ResponseTextDeltaEvent, response_markdown: ui.markdown):
58	    """
59	    Handle a text delta event by updating the response markdown and scrolling the history.
60	    """
61	    response_markdown.content += event.data.delta
62	    state.history.scroll_to(percent=100)
63	    await asyncio.sleep(0)
64	
65	async def handle_handoff_event(state: APPState, event: AgentUpdatedStreamEvent, divider: ui.row):
66	    logger.info(f"\n[Agent switched to: {event.new_agent.name}]")
67	    # TODO: If we use handoffs, then this needs to be updated so that we get a history of the handoffs
68	
69	async def handle_tool_call_event(state: APPState, event: RunItemStreamEvent, divider: ui.row):
70	    """
71	    This event occurs when the agent calls a tool.   Handle a tool call event by updating the response
72	    markdown with the tool call details.
73	    """
74	    raw_item = event.item.raw_item
75	    tool_name = raw_item.name
76	    args = raw_item.arguments
77	    thought = f"🔧 Calling tool **{tool_name}** with arguments:\n```\n{args}\n```"
78	    logger.debug(thought)
79	    with thoughts_container(divider):
80	        with ui.chat_message(name='Tool Call', sent=False).classes('w-full'):
81	            ui.markdown(f"{tool_name}({args})")
82	
83	async def handle_tool_output_event(state: APPState, event: RunItemStreamEvent, divider: ui.row):
84	    """
85	    This event occurs when a tool responds to a tool call event.   Handle the event by updating the 
86	    "thoughts" container with the tool output.
87	    """
88	    item = event.item
89	    output = item.output
90	    thought = f"📤 Tool responded with:\n```\n{output}\n```"
91	    logger.debug(thought)
92	    with thoughts_container(divider):
93	        with ui.chat_message(name='Tool Output', sent=False).classes('w-full'):
94	            ui.markdown(str(output))
95	
96	
97	async def handle_message_output_event(state: APPState, event: RunItemStreamEvent, divider: ui.row):
98	    """
99	    This event occurs after a tool output is received and the agent generates a message based on the tool ouput.
100	    Handle the event by updating the response markdown with the final message.
101	    """
102	    item = event.item
103	    text = ItemHelpers.text_message_output(item)
104	    thought = f"🧠 Using tool output to generate message"
105	    logger.debug(thought)

107	async def handle_agent_events(state: APPState, messages: list[dict], response_markdown: ui.markdown, divider: ui.row):
108	    agents = state.agents
109	    selection = state.selection
110	    kind = "home" if not selection else selection[-1].kind
111	    agent = agents.get(kind, None)
112	    logger.debug(f"Handling agent events for kind: {kind}")
113	    if agent is None:
114	        raise ValueError(f"Agent not found for kind: {kind}")
115	    stream = Runner.run_streamed(agent, input=messages, context=state)
116	    streamed_events = stream.stream_events()
117	    async for event in streamed_events:
118	        # --- RAW TEXT TOKENS ---
119	        if event.type == "raw_response_event" and isinstance(event.data, ResponseTextDeltaEvent):
120	            await handle_text_delta_event(state, event, response_markdown)
121	        elif event.type == "agent_updated_stream_event":
122	            await handle_handoff_event(state, event, response_markdown)
123	
124	        # --- TOOL USAGE EVENTS ---
125	        elif event.type == "run_item_stream_event":
126	            item = event.item
127	
128	            if item.type == "tool_call_item":
129	                await handle_tool_call_event(state, event, divider)
130	                
131	            # Tool output
132	            elif item.type == "tool_call_output_item":
133	                await handle_tool_output_event(state, event, divider)
134	
135	            # Completed LLM message (post-tool or final)
136	            elif item.type == "message_output_item":
137	                await handle_message_output_event(state, event, divider)
138	            else:
139	                msg = f"Unhandled item type: {item.type} while using tools"
140	                logger.error(item.type)
141	                
142	    return stream.to_input_list()
143

145	async def send(state: APPState):
146	    # Dereference state variables
147	    history = state.history
148	    text_input = state.user_input
149	    question = text_input.value
150	    text_input.value = ''
151	
152	    # Capture image editor selection if we're in that view
153	    try:
154	        if state.selection and state.selection[-1].kind.value == "image-editor" and state.image_editor_dom_id:
155	            js = f"window.__imageEditorSelections && window.__imageEditorSelections['{state.image_editor_dom_id}']"
156	            selection_data = await ui.run_javascript(js)
157	            if selection_data:
158	                state.image_editor_selection = selection_data
159	    except Exception as e:
160	        logger.debug(f"Failed to read image editor selection: {e}")
161	
162	    # TODO: Disable the "send button" while the response is being generated
163	
164	    # Build The Message History
165	    messages = state.get_messages(role_map=ROLE_MAP)
166	    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": question})
167	
168	    # Post the question to the message history
169	    with state.history:
170	        append_history('You', question, sent=True)
171	
172	    # Create a container for internal thoughts
173	        divider = ui.row().classes('w-full')
174	
175	    # Initialize the UI elements for the response message handling
176	        with ui.chat_message(name='Bot', sent=False).classes('w-full'):
177	            with ui.column().classes('w-full'):
178	                response_markdown = ui.markdown("").classes('w-full')
179	                spinner = ui.spinner('dots', size="2em")
180	    
181	    history.scroll_to(percent=100)
182	    
183	    # Stream the responses from the agent, updating the UI as we go
184	    responses = await handle_agent_events(state, messages, response_markdown, divider)
185	
186	    # Now that we are done, celan up the ui.
187	    # TODO: RE-enable "send button" after the response is complete
188	    spinner.delete()
189	    
190	    state.write()
191	    if state.is_dirty:
192	        state.refresh_details()
193	        state.is_dirty = False

2	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
3	from pydantic import BaseModel
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from schema import *
6	
7	class GenericStorage(ABC):
8	    """
9	    Abstract base class for generic storage systems.
10	    """
11	
12	    @abstractmethod
13	    def create_object(self, data: BaseModel, overwrite: bool=False) -> str:
14	        """
15	        Create a new object in the specified path with the given data.
16	
17	        Args:
18	            data (BaseModel): The data to create the object with.
19	            overwrite (bool): Whether to overwrite an existing object with the same ID. Defaults to False.
20	                If this is false, the ID will be replaced with a locally unique identifier.
21	        """
22	        pass
23	
24	    @abstractmethod
25	    def read_object(self, cls: BaseModel, primary_key: dict[str,str]={}) -> Optional[BaseModel]:
26	        """
27	        Read an object from a file and return it as an instance of the specified class.
28	
29	        Args:
30	            cls (BaseModel): The class to which the object should be converted.
31	            primary_key (dict[str,str]): The primary key of the object to read.   This is used to
32	              construct the filepath to the object.
33	        """
34	        pass
35	            
36	    @abstractmethod
37	    def read_all_objects(self, cls: BaseModel, primary_key: dict[str,str] = {}, order_by: Optional[Union[str, Callable[[BaseModel], str]]] = None) -> list[BaseModel]:
38	        """
39	        Read all objects from a directory and return them as a list of instances of the specified class.
40	        
41	        Args:
42	            cls (BaseModel): The class to which the objects should be converted.
43	            primary_key (dict[str,str]): The primary key of the parent object (if any).   This is used to
44	              construct the filepath to the object.
45	            order_by: An optional key that represents the field in which the instances will be returned
46	        """
47	        pass
48	        
49	    @abstractmethod
50	    def update_object(self, data: BaseModel) -> None:
51	        """
52	        Update an existing object in the specified file with the given data.
53	        
54	        Args:
55	            filepath (str): The path to the file containing the object.
56	            data (BaseModel): The data to update the object with.
57	        """
58	        pass
59	
60	    @abstractmethod
61	    def delete_object(self, cls: BaseModel, primary_key: dict[str,str]) -> Optional[BaseModel]:
62	        """
63	        Delete an object from a file.   If it existed, then return the object so
64	        that it could be used for further processing (e.g. logging, undoing, unlinking, etc).
65	
66	        Args:
67	            filepath (str): The path to the file containing the object.
68	            delete_folder (bool): Whether to delete the folder containing the object. Defaults to True.
69	            cls (BaseModel): The class of the object to be deleted.
70	        """
71	        pass
72	
73	    @abstractmethod
74	    def list_images(self, obj: BaseModel) -> list[str]:
75	        """
76	        List all images associated with a given object.
77	
78	        Args:
79	            obj (BaseModel): The object for which to list images.
80	
81	        Returns:
82	            list[str]: A list of image file paths.
83	        """
84	        pass
85	
86	    @abstractmethod
87	    def find_image(self, obj: BaseModel, locator: str) -> Optional[str]:
88	        """
89	        Find an image associated with a given object.
90	        Args:
91	            obj (BaseModel): The object for which to find the image.
92	            locator (str): The locator of the image.
93	        Returns:
94	            Optional[str]: The file path of the image if found, otherwise None.
95	        """
96	        pass
97	
98	    @abstractmethod
99	    def list_uploads(self, obj: BaseModel) -> list[str]:
100	        """
101	        List all uploads associated with a given object.
102	
103	        Args:
104	            obj (BaseModel): The object for which to list uploads.
105	
106	        Returns:
107	            list[str]: A list of upload file paths.
108	        """
109	        pass
110	
111	    @abstractmethod
112	    def upload_image(self, obj: BaseModel, name: str, data: BinaryIO, mime_type: str) -> str:
113	        """
114	        Upload an image for a given object.
115	
116	        Args:
117	            obj (BaseModel): The object to which the image belongs.
118	            name (str): The name of the image file.
119	            data (BinaryIO): The binary data of the image.
120	            mime_type (str): The MIME type of the image.
121	
122	        Returns:
123	            str: The file locator of the uploaded image.
124	        """
125	        pass
126	
127	    @abstractmethod
128	    def upload_reference_image(self, obj: BaseModel, name: str, data: BinaryIO, mime_type: str) -> str:
129	        """
130	        Upload a reference image for a given object.
131	
132	        Args:
133	            obj (BaseModel): The object to which the image belongs.
134	            name (str): The name of the image file.
135	            data (BinaryIO): The binary data of the image.
136	            mime_type (str): The MIME type of the image.
137	
138	        Returns:
139	            str: The file locator of the uploaded image.
140	        """
141	        pass
142	
143	    @abstractmethod
144	    def upload_binary_image(self, obj: BaseModel, data: bytes) -> str:
145	        """
146	        Upload a binary image for a given object.
147	
148	        Args:
149	            obj (BaseModel): The object to which the image belongs.
150	            data (bytes): The binary data of the image.
151	
152	        Returns:
153	            str: The file locator of the uploaded image.
154	        """
155	        pass
156	
157	    @abstractmethod
158	    def delete_image(self, locator: str) -> None:
159	        """
160	        Delete an image given its locator
161	
162	        Args:
163	            locator: The locator for the image
164	
165	        """
166	        pass
167	
168	    @abstractmethod
169	    def find_issue_image(self, series_id: str, issue_id: str) -> str | None:
170	        """
171	        get the image locator for a representative image for an issue.  This will 
172	        choose the front cover, if it exists, otherwise will return the first
173	        of the inside_front, inside_back and back covers.   If none exist, it will
174	        return none.
175	        """
176	        pass

44	def init_layout(logger):
45	    """
46	    Initializes the UI layout with the main components.
47	    This includes the header, middle, and footer regions.   The ui elements are layed out
48	    as depicted below:
49	
50	    +----------------------------------------------------------+
51	    | Breadcrumbs                             Dark Mode Switch |  Header Region
52	    +----------------------------------------------------------+
53	    |  details                   |    history                  |  Middle Region
54	    | (Current Selection)        | (Conversation History)      |
55	    +----------------------------------------------------------+
56	    | User Input Field and Send Button                         |  Footer Region
57	    +----------------------------------------------------------+
58	    """
59	    # SET THE DARK MODE BASED ON THE ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE
60	    dark_mode = os.getenv('DARK_MODE', 'False').lower() in ['true', '1', 'yes']
61	    # INITIALIZE THE WINDOW LAYOUT
62	    ui.query('.nicegui-content').classes('w-full')
63	    ui.query('.q-page').classes('flex')   
64	    header = ui.header().classes().classes(HEADFOOT_STYLING_CLASSES)
65	    middle = ui.row().classes('w-screen flex-1 overflow-hidden' + MIDDLE_STYLING_CLASSES).style('padding-left:12px; padding-right:12px;')
66	    footer = ui.footer().classes('bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-300')
67	
68	    # INITIALIZE EACH OF THE REGIONS
69	    # Header region has breadcrums for navigation and the dark mode switch
70	    with header:
71	        breadcrumbs = ui.button_group()
72	        ui.space()
73	        darkswitch = ui.switch('Dark mode')
74	
75	
76	    # Middle region has the details and history sections, side by side with a movable splitter
77	    # The details shows the current selection while the history shows the conversation history
78	    with middle:
79	        with ui.splitter(limits=(20,80), value=70).classes('h-full w-full') as splitter:
80	            with splitter.before:
81	                details = ui.scroll_area().classes("h-full w-full "+MIDDLE_STYLING_CLASSES).style('padding-left:12px; padding-right:12px;')
82	            with splitter.after:
83	                history = ui.scroll_area().classes("h-full w-full"+MIDDLE_STYLING_CLASSES+" border").style('padding-left:12px; padding-right:12px;')
84	
85	    # Footer region has the user input field and the send button
86	    with footer:
87	        placeholder = "message"
88	        user_input = ui.input(placeholder=placeholder).props('rounded outlined input-class=mx-3 ') \
89	            .classes('w-full self-center stt-input').style('flex-grow: 1; width: 100vh;')
90	
91	        mic_button = ui.button('Dictate').props('rounded').classes('q-ml-md')
92	        stop_mic_button = ui.button('Stop').props('rounded').classes('q-ml-md')
93	        speak_button = ui.button('Speak').props('rounded').classes('q-ml-md')
94	        stop_speak_button = ui.button('Mute').props('rounded').classes('q-ml-md')
95	        send_button = ui.button('Send').props('rounded').classes('q-ml-md')
96	
97	    return (
98	        breadcrumbs,
99	        details,
100	        history,
101	        user_input,
102	        send_button,
103	        darkswitch,
104	        mic_button,
105	        stop_mic_button,
106	        speak_button,
107	        stop_speak_button,
108	    )
109	
110	
111	@ui.page('/')

113	    # INITIALIZE THE LOGGER
114	    init_logger()
115	
116	    # INITIALIZE THE UI LAYOUT WITH BASIC ELEMENTS
117	    (
118	        breadcrumbs,
119	        details,
120	        history,
121	        user_input,
122	        send_button,
123	        darkswitch,
124	        mic_button,

2	This module handles the messaging functionality for the application, including sending messages,

5	are posed to a special "thoughts" container that can be expanded by the user to see the details.

35	    """

38	    # if we’ve already created it, reuse it
39	    if hasattr(parent, "_thoughts_expand"):
40	        return parent._thoughts_expand
41	
42	    # otherwise create it and stash it on the parent
43	    with parent:
44	        parent._thoughts_expand = (
45	            ui.expansion("Thoughts", value=False)
46	              .classes("w-full text-sm")
47	        )

98	    """
99	    This event occurs after a tool output is received and the agent generates a message based on the tool ouput.
100	    Handle the event by updating the response markdown with the final message.

107	async def handle_agent_events(state: APPState, messages: list[dict], response_markdown: ui.markdown, divider: ui.row):
108	    agents = state.agents
109	    selection = state.selection
110	    kind = "home" if not selection else selection[-1].kind
111	    agent = agents.get(kind, None)
112	    logger.debug(f"Handling agent events for kind: {kind}")
113	    if agent is None:
114	        raise ValueError(f"Agent not found for kind: {kind}")
115	    stream = Runner.run_streamed(agent, input=messages, context=state)
116	    streamed_events = stream.stream_events()
117	    async for event in streamed_events:
118	        # --- RAW TEXT TOKENS ---
119	        if event.type == "raw_response_event" and isinstance(event.data, ResponseTextDeltaEvent):
120	            await handle_text_delta_event(state, event, response_markdown)
121	        elif event.type == "agent_updated_stream_event":
122	            await handle_handoff_event(state, event, response_markdown)
123	
124	        # --- TOOL USAGE EVENTS ---
125	        elif event.type == "run_item_stream_event":
126	            item = event.item
127	
128	            if item.type == "tool_call_item":
129	                await handle_tool_call_event(state, event, divider)
130	                
131	            # Tool output
132	            elif item.type == "tool_call_output_item":
133	                await handle_tool_output_event(state, event, divider)
134	
135	            # Completed LLM message (post-tool or final)
136	            elif item.type == "message_output_item":
137	                await handle_message_output_event(state, event, divider)
138	            else:
139	                msg = f"Unhandled item type: {item.type} while using tools"
140	                logger.error(item.type)
141	                
142	    return stream.to_input_list()
143

145	async def send(state: APPState):
146	    # Dereference state variables
147	    history = state.history
148	    text_input = state.user_input
149	    question = text_input.value
150	    text_input.value = ''
151	
152	    # Capture image editor selection if we're in that view
153	    try:
154	        if state.selection and state.selection[-1].kind.value == "image-editor" and state.image_editor_dom_id:
155	            js = f"window.__imageEditorSelections && window.__imageEditorSelections['{state.image_editor_dom_id}']"
156	            selection_data = await ui.run_javascript(js)
157	            if selection_data:
158	                state.image_editor_selection = selection_data
159	    except Exception as e:
160	        logger.debug(f"Failed to read image editor selection: {e}")
161	
162	    # TODO: Disable the "send button" while the response is being generated
163	
164	    # Build The Message History
165	    messages = state.get_messages(role_map=ROLE_MAP)
166	    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": question})
167	
168	    # Post the question to the message history
169	    with state.history:
170	        append_history('You', question, sent=True)
171	
172	    # Create a container for internal thoughts
173	        divider = ui.row().classes('w-full')
174	
175	    # Initialize the UI elements for the response message handling
176	        with ui.chat_message(name='Bot', sent=False).classes('w-full'):
177	            with ui.column().classes('w-full'):
178	                response_markdown = ui.markdown("").classes('w-full')
179	                spinner = ui.spinner('dots', size="2em")
180	    
181	    history.scroll_to(percent=100)
182	    
183	    # Stream the responses from the agent, updating the UI as we go
184	    responses = await handle_agent_events(state, messages, response_markdown, divider)
185	
186	    # Now that we are done, celan up the ui.
187	    # TODO: RE-enable "send button" after the response is complete
188	    spinner.delete()
189	    
190	    state.write()
191	    if state.is_dirty:
192	        state.refresh_details()
193	        state.is_dirty = False

20	# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	# Functions to read objects from the database.   These are used by the tools
22	# To perform the file operations
23	# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	def read_all(
25	        wrapper: RunContextWrapper[APPState], 
26	        cls: type[BaseModel], 
27	        parent_key: dict[str, str] | None = None,
28	        order_by: Optional[Union[str, Callable[[BaseModel], str]]] = None
29	        ) -> list[BaseModel]:
30	    """
31	    Read all objects of a given class from the database.   
32	
33	    Args:
34	        wrapper: The run context wrapper containing the application state.
35	        cls: The class of the objects to read.
36	        parent_key: An optional primary key for the parent object.   If not provided, the function will
37	            read all the objects rooted on the current selection.
38	    Notes: This function throws if any of the objects are ill formed.
39	    """
40	    state: APPState = wrapper.context
41	    storage:GenericStorage = state.storage
42	
43	    logger.trace(f"Finding all objects of type {cls.__name__}")
44	    context = selection_to_context(state.selection)
45	
46	    CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG = f"Cannot find {cls.__name__} instances."
47	
48	    if len(context) == 0 and cls.__name__ not in ["Series", "Publisher", "ComicStyle"]:
49	        logger.error(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)
50	        raise ValueError(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)
51	
52	    if len(context) == 0:
53	        return storage.read_all_objects(cls=cls)
54	
55	    # If we reach here, the requested object is a child of the last item in the hierarchy
56	    pk_parent = context[0][1] if parent_key is None else parent_key
57	    objs = storage.read_all_objects(cls=cls, primary_key=pk_parent)
58	
59	    if order_by is None:
60	        return objs
61	    elif callable(order_by):
62	        return sorted(objs, key=order_by)
63	    elif isinstance(order_by, str):
64	        return sorted(objs, key=lambda obj: getattr(obj, order_by))
65	    else:
66	        logger.error(f"Invalid order_by argument: {order_by}")
67	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid order_by argument: {order_by}")
68	
69	
70	def read_one(wrapper: RunContextWrapper[APPState], cls: type[BaseModel], pk: dict[str, str]) -> BaseModel:
71	    """
72	    Find an object in the storage by its primary key.
73	    
74	    Args:
75	        pk: The primary key of the object to find.
76	    
77	    Returns:
78	        The object if found, otherwise a status message.
79	    """
80	    state: APPState = wrapper.context
81	    storage: GenericStorage = state.storage
82	
83	    logger.trace(f"Finding object with key = {pk}")
84	    context = selection_to_context(state.selection)
85	
86	    CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG = f"Cannot find object with key = {pk}.   It is not in the context of the current selection.  You may need to change the current selection."
87	    NOT_FOUND_ERROR_MSG = f"Cannot find object with key = {pk}.   It is not in the database.   You may want to verify the primary key(s)"
88	    TOP_LEVEL_IDS = ['series_id', 'publisher_id', 'style_id']
89	
90	    pk_keys = list(pk.keys())
91	
92	    if len(pk_keys)==1 and pk_keys[0] in TOP_LEVEL_IDS:
93	        # Special case: Top level ids can be retrieved regardless of the current context.
94	        logger.debug("Finding top level object with key = {pk}")
95	    elif len(context) == 0:
96	        # If the context is empty, we can still find series, publishers and styles (since they)
97	        # are top level objects in the hierarchy.
98	        if any(pk.keys(), lambda k: k not in TOP_LEVEL_IDS):
99	            # However if the user selected something that is not at the top level of the hierarch, then
100	            # We throw an error
101	            logger.error(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)
102	            raise ValueError(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)
103	        logger.debug("Finding top level object with key = {pk}")
104	
105	    # Special case for when the context and the primary key of the current selection are the same
106	    elif len(context) > 0 and context[0][1] == pk:
107	        logger.debug(f"Finding current selection {pk}")
108	    
109	    # The requested object should be a child of the selected object
110	    elif len(context) > 0 and len(pk) == len(context)+1:
111	        parent_pk = context[0][1]
112	        parent_keys = list(parent_pk.keys())
113	        child_keys = pk.keys()
114	        while len(parent_keys) > 0:
115	            k = parent_keys.pop()
116	            if k not in child_keys:
117	                logger.error(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)
118	                raise ValueError(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)
119	            if pk[k] != parent_pk[k]:
120	                logger.error(CONTEXT_ERROR_MSG)

23	def select_publisher(wrapper: RunContextWrapper[APPState], publisher_id: str) -> str:
24	    """
25	    Select a publisher by identifier.   This is a precursor for editing its 
26	    properties.
27	    
28	    Args:
29	        publisher_id: The identifier of the publisher to select.
30	
31	    Returns:
32	        A status message indicating the result of the selection.
33	    """
34	    state: APPState = wrapper.context
35	    storage: GenericStorage = state.storage
36	
37	    publisher: Publisher = storage.read_object(cls=Publisher, primary_key={"publisher_id": publisher_id})
38	    if publisher is None:
39	        return f"Publisher '{publisher_id}' not found.  Maybe try looking at the list of publishers first?"
40	    sel_itm = SelectionItem(
41	        id=publisher.publisher_id,
42	        name=publisher.name,
43	        kind=SelectedKind.PUBLISHER,
44	    )
45	    new_selection = state.selection + [sel_itm]
46	    state.change_selection(new=new_selection)
47	    return f"Selected publisher: {publisher.publisher_id}"

55	    Args:

30	class LocalStorage(GenericStorage):
31	
32	    def __init__(self, base_path: str):
33	        super().__init__()
34	        self.base_path = base_path
35	        if not os.path.exists(self.base_path):
36	            os.makedirs(self.base_path)
37	
38	    def create_object(self, data: BaseModel, overwrite: bool=False) -> str:
39	        """
40	        Create a new object in the specified path with the given data.   This new object will
41	        be assigned a unique ID, and will be stored in a file using the object's 
42	        FILEPATH_TEMPLATE.
43	
44	        Args:
45	            data (BaseModel): The data to create the object with.   Strictly speaking, this
46	            should be an instance of a class that has a primary_key, parent_key and id 
47	            property.
48	
49	        Returns:
50	            str: The unique ID of the created object.
51	        """
52	        logger.trace(f"creating {data.__class__.__name__} with data: {data.model_dump()}")
53	
54	        # Determine the root path of the object by using the appropriate path templates.
55	        # If the object is not a valid class, then this method throws an error.
56	        rootpath = obj_to_rootpath(data)
57	
58	        # Verify that the rootpath exists and is a directory.   If it does not exist, then
59	        # create it.   If it exists but is not a directory, then raise an error.
60	        if not os.path.exists(rootpath):
61	            os.makedirs(rootpath)        
62	        if not os.path.isdir(rootpath):
63	            raise NotADirectoryError(f"Path {rootpath} is not a directory.")
64	        
65	        # Get the object's identifier field name.   This field will need to be set to ensure
66	        # That the primary key is globally unique.   If the field is not found then raise
67	        # an error.
68	        id_field = get_object_id_field_name(data)
69	        if id_field is None:
70	            msg = f"Object {data.__class__.__name__} does not have an identifier field."
71	        if not hasattr(data, id_field):
72	            msg = f"Object {data.__class__.__name__} does not have an id field named {id_field}."
73	            logger.error(msg)
74	            raise ValueError(msg)
75	
76	        # If we get here, we know that the rootpath exists and is a directory, and that the
77	        # object has an identifier field.   We can now generate a unique ID for the object
78	        # and set the id field to that ID.   
79	        if not overwrite:
80	            obj_id = generate_unique_id(path=rootpath, create_folder=True)
81	            setattr(data, id_field, obj_id)  # Set the id field to the generated unique ID
82	        else:
83	            obj_id = getattr(data, id_field)
84	        
85	        filepath = obj_to_filepath(data)  # This will raise an error if the object is not valid
86	        parent_path = os.path.dirname(filepath)
87	        # make sure that file's folder exists
88	        if not os.path.exists(parent_path):
89	            os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(filepath))
90	        if not os.path.isdir(parent_path):
91	            raise NotADirectoryError(f"Path {parent_path} is not a directory.")
92	
93	        # Write the object to the file in JSON format.   The file will be created if it does not exist.
94	        logger.debug(f"Writing object {data.__class__.__name__} to file {filepath}")
95	        with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
96	            f.write(data.model_dump_json(indent=2))
97	            f.flush()
98	            os.fsync(f.fileno())
99	
100	        # sync the parent folder
101	        fs_dir = os.open(parent_path, os.O_DIRECTORY)
102	        os.fsync(fs_dir)
103	        os.close(fs_dir)
104	
105	        # Return the unique ID of the created object.
106	        return obj_id
107	
108	    def read_object(self, cls: BaseModel, primary_key: dict[str,str]={}) -> Optional[BaseModel]:
109	        """
110	        Read an object from a file and return it as an instance of the specified class.
111	
112	        Args:
113	            cls (BaseModel): The class to which the object should be converted.
114	            primary_key (dict[str,str]): The primary key of the object to read.   This is used to
115	              construct the filepath to the object.
116	        """
117	        logger.trace(f"Reading {cls.__name__} object with primary key: {primary_key}")
118	        filepath = cls_to_filepath(cls=cls, pk=primary_key)
119	
120	
121	        if not os.path.exists(filepath):
122	            logger.debug(f"File {filepath} does not exist. Returning None.")
123	            return None
124	        
125	        if not os.path.isfile(filepath):
126	            logger.error(f"Path {filepath} is not a file.")
127	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Path {filepath} is not a file.")
128	        
129	        with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
130	            data = json.load(f)
131	            f.flush()
132	            os.fsync(f.fileno())
133	
134	        # sync the parent folder
135	        parent_path = os.path.dirname(filepath)
136	        fs_dir = os.open(parent_path, os.O_DIRECTORY)
137	        os.fsync(fs_dir)
138	        os.close(fs_dir)
139	
140	        try: 
141	            return cls.model_validate(data)
142	        except Exception as e:
143	            msg = f"Failed to validate: {e}"
144	            logger.error(msg)
145	            raise
146	
147	    def read_all_objects(self, cls: BaseModel, primary_key: dict[str,str] = {}, order_by: Optional[Union[str, Callable[[BaseModel], str]]] = None) -> list[BaseModel]:
148	        """
149	        Read all objects from a directory and return them as a list of instances of the specified class.
150	        
151	        Args:
152	            cls (BaseModel): The class to which the objects should be converted.
153	            primary_key (dict[str,str]): The primary key of the parent object (if any).   This is used to
154	              construct the filepath to the object.
155	        """
156	        logger.trace(f"Reading all {cls.__name__} objects relative to primary key {primary_key}")
157	        # Determine the root path and filepath template for the class
158	        rootpath_template = ROOT_PATH_TEMPLATES.get(cls.__name__, None)
159	        filepath_template = FILEPATH_TEMPLATES.get(cls.__name__, None)
160	        if rootpath_template is None:
161	            msg = f"Root path template for class {cls.__name__} not found."
162	            logger.error(msg)
163	            raise ValueError(msg)
164	        if filepath_template is None:
165	            msg = f"Filepath template for class {cls.__name__} not found."
166	            logger.error(msg)
167	            raise ValueError(msg)
168	        rootpath_template: str = rootpath_template
169	        filepath_template: str = filepath_template
170	
171	        # There should be exactly one key that is in the filepath template that is not
172	        # in the primary key.   We need to know which key is missing so that we can
173	        # Can populate that with the correct value later.
174	        rootpath_keys = extract_format_keys(rootpath_template)
175	        filepath_keys = extract_format_keys(filepath_template)
176	        missing_keys = filepath_keys - rootpath_keys
177	
178	        if len(missing_keys) != 1:
179	            msg = f"Expected exactly one missing key in the rootpath template {rootpath_template} for class {cls.__name__}.   Found {len(missing_keys)} missing keys: {missing_keys}."
180	            logger.error(msg)
181	            raise ValueError(msg)
182	        missing_key = list(missing_keys)[0]
183	
184	        rootpath = template_to_filepath(template=rootpath_template, pk=primary_key)
185	
186	        if not os.path.exists(rootpath):
187	            logger.debug(f"Path {rootpath} does not exist. Returning empty list.")
188	            return []
189	        
190	        if not os.path.isdir(rootpath):
191	            msg = f"Path {rootpath} is not a directory."
192	            logger.error(msg)
193	            raise NotADirectoryError(msg)
194	        
195	        
196	        objects = []
197	        # Get all the non-hidden folders in the path
198	        subfolders = get_basenames(rootpath)
199	        for folder in subfolders:
200	            obj = self.read_object(cls, primary_key={**primary_key, missing_key: folder})
201	            if obj:
202	                objects.append(obj)
203	        logger.debug(f"Found {len(objects)} objects of type {cls.__name__} in {rootpath}")
204	
205	        if order_by is None:
206	            return objects
207	        elif isinstance(order_by, str):
208	            objects.sort(key=lambda x: getattr(x, order_by))
209	        elif callable(order_by):
210	            objects.sort(key=order_by)
211	        else:
212	            msg = f"order_by must be a string or a callable, got {type(order_by)}"
213	            logger.error(msg)
214	            raise ValueError(msg)
215	        return objects
216	        
217	
218	    def update_object(self, data: BaseModel) -> None:
219	        """
220	        Update an existing object in the specified file with the given data.
221	        
222	        Args:
223	            filepath (str): The path to the file containing the object.
224	            data (BaseModel): The data to update the object with.
225	        """
226	        logger.trace(f"Updating {data.__class__.__name__} with data: {data.model_dump()}")
227	        filepath = obj_to_filepath(data)  # This will raise an error if the object is not valid
228	        
229	        if not os.path.exists(filepath):
230	            logger.error(f"File {filepath} does not exist. Cannot update.")
231	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"File {filepath} does not exist.")
232	        
233	        if not os.path.isfile(filepath):
234	            logger.error(f"Path {filepath} is not a file.")
235	            raise NotADirectoryError(f"Path {filepath} is not a file.")
236	        
237	        with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
238	            f.write(json.dumps(data.model_dump(), indent=2))
239	            f.flush()
240	            os.fsync(f.fileno())
241	
242	        # sync the parent folder
243	        parent_path = os.path.dirname(filepath)
244	        fs_dir = os.open(parent_path, os.O_DIRECTORY)
245	        os.fsync(fs_dir)
246	        os.close(fs_dir)
247	
248	    def delete_object(self, cls: BaseModel, primary_key: dict[str,str]) -> Optional[BaseModel]:
249	        """
250	        Delete an object from a file.   If it existed, then return the object so
251	        that it could be used for further processing (e.g. logging, undoing, unlinking, etc).
252	
253	        Args:
254	            filepath (str): The path to the file containing the object.
255	            delete_folder (bool): Whether to delete the folder containing the object. Defaults to True.
256	            cls (BaseModel): The class of the object to be deleted.
257	        """
258	        
259	        logger.trace(f"Deleting {cls.__name__} with primary key: {primary_key}")
260	        instance = self.read_object(cls=cls, primary_key=primary_key)
261	        logger.debug(f"Instance to delete: {instance}")
262	        if instance is None:
263	            logger.debug(f"File {cls.__name__} does not exist. Cannot delete.")
264	            return None
265	        
266	        filepath = obj_to_filepath(instance)
267	        logger.debug(f"Deleting file at {filepath}")
268	        shutil.rmtree(os.path.dirname(filepath), ignore_errors=True)
269	
270	        # sync the grandparent folder
271	        grandparent_path = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(filepath))
272	        fs_dir = os.open(grandparent_path, os.O_DIRECTORY)
273	        os.fsync(fs_dir)
274	        os.close(fs_dir)
275	
276	        return instance
277	    
278	    def _list_images(self, path: str) -> list[str]:
279	        logger.debug(f"Images path: {path}")
280	        images = []
281	        # If the folder does not exist, return an empty list
282	        if not os.path.exists(path):
283	            return images
284	        if not os.path.isdir(path):
285	            logger.error(f"Path {path} is not a directory.")
286	            raise NotADirectoryError(f"Path {path} is not a directory.")
287	        # If the image exists, then return all the image files in the folder.
288

1	# Vision
2	
3	Build a creator‑first comic production workspace where writers and artists can develop a series end‑to‑end: worldbuilding, characters, issues, scenes, panels, and final imagery, with AI assistance that accelerates iteration without removing creative control. The AI is another voice in the room—a collaborator that can brainstorm and a critic that can challenge choices when useful.
4	
5	## What We Are Building
6	
7	A single workspace that blends:
8	- Structured story data (series → issues → scenes → panels).
9	- Visual systems (styles, character variants, reference images).
10	- AI tools for drafting, refinement, and image generation.
11	- A visual UI for browsing, editing, and selecting assets.
12	
13	The goal is to make comic creation feel like a studio workflow—organized, iterative, and fast—while preserving the creator’s voice and decisions.
14	
15	## Core Workflow
16	
17	1. Define a series and its core premise.
18	2. Create characters and variants with visual references.
19	3. Draft an issue summary.
20	4. Break the issue into scenes (story + setting).
21	5. Generate panel drafts from scene story beats.
22	6. Refine panel descriptions, dialogue, and composition.
23	7. Generate panel/cover images with consistent style references.
24	8. Review, select, and organize final assets.
25	
26	## Product Experience (Current Build)
27	
28	The app is a studio‑style workspace that pairs structured editing with a conversational AI assistant. It is designed to keep the creator focused on one object at a time while maintaining full context through breadcrumbs and history.
29	
30	- Header: breadcrumbs for navigation and a dark‑mode toggle.
31	- Split view: left side shows details for the current selection; right side shows conversation history with the AI.
32	- Footer: chat input and send button used to request creation, editing, and rendering.
33	
34	## Navigation & Selection Model
35	
36	Creators move through a hierarchical workspace using clickable cards and breadcrumbs. Selecting any item rehydrates the details panel with that object’s fields, images, and actions.
37	
38	- Home shows three entry points: Series, Styles, and Publishers.
39	- Series contains Issues and Characters.
40	- Characters contain Variants and styled images.
41	- Issues contain Scenes and Covers.
42	- Scenes contain Panels.
43	- Panels and Covers contain images, character references, and reference uploads.
44	
45	## Creation & Editing Loop
46	
47	Every field has lightweight CRUD actions. These actions post a short request into the chat, where the AI performs the creation or edit and the UI refreshes in place.
48	
49	- Create/edit buttons live in section headers and field editors.
50	- Render buttons are attached to image grids to request AI image generation.
51	- Deletion is explicit and isolated to the current selection.
52	
53	## Workflow (Current Build)
54	
55	1. Create a comic style, including art style, character style, and dialog styles. Generate style examples as visual anchors.
56	2. Optionally create a publisher and a logo reference.
57	3. Create a series and assign a publisher.
58	4. Add characters, then add variants with detailed appearance and behavior notes.
59	5. Generate styled images for variants to lock visual consistency.
60	6. Create an issue with a story summary, set its style, and fill in issue metadata.
61	7. Create scenes with narrative summaries and style.
62	8. Create panels from each scene: define beat, visual description, aspect ratio, and add dialogue/narration.
63	9. Create covers with descriptions, character references, and aspect ratio.
64	10. Render images for panels and covers, then select the final image in each grid.
65	
66	## Image & Reference Workflow
67	
68	Images are managed per object with a selectable grid. Reference images are separate from final selections and are used to guide AI generation.
69	
70	- Panels, covers, and styles support image grids with upload, select, render, and delete.
71	- Reference images can be uploaded to panels and covers and then selected for use.
72	- Scenes allow a direct “drop image” flow to seed new panels.
73	
74	## UX Diagrams
75	
76	Layout and flow are represented as simple system diagrams to keep the experience aligned across UI and AI behavior.
77	
78	```mermaid
79	flowchart LR
80	  H["Header (Breadcrumbs + Dark Mode)"] --- M["Main Split View"]
81	  M --- L["Details Panel (Current Selection)"]
82	  M --- R["History Panel (Conversation)"]
83	  F["Footer (Chat Input + Send)"] --- M
84	```
85	
86	```mermaid
87	flowchart TD
88	  HOME["Home"] --> SERIES["Series"]
89	  HOME --> STYLES["Styles"]
90	  HOME --> PUBLISHERS["Publishers"]
91	
92	  SERIES --> ISSUE["Issue"]
93	  SERIES --> CHARACTER["Character"]
94	  CHARACTER --> VARIANT["Variant"]
95	  VARIANT --> STYLED_IMG["Styled Images"]
96	
97	  ISSUE --> SCENE["Scene"]
98	  ISSUE --> COVER["Cover"]
99	  SCENE --> PANEL["Panel"]
100

1	# Task List
2	
3	## Core Product Scope
4	- Define canonical product scope for “multiuser, AI-first comic authoring and publishing” and align `docs/vision.md` and `vision.md`.
5	- Decide the single source of truth for product docs and de-duplicate or redirect the other vision file.
6	- Specify target personas (writer, artist, editor, publisher admin) and success metrics per persona.
7	- Define MVP vs. v1 vs. v2 feature slices for planning.
8	
9	## Critical Bugs & Code Fixes
10	- Fix `agentic/tools/reader.py` invalid `any(pk.keys(), ...)` usage and top-level key logic.
11	- Fix `agentic/tools/navigation.py` to use `state.change_selection` rather than mutating `state.selection`.
12	- Fix `messaging.py` to append tool-driven responses in `handle_message_output_event`.
13	- Fix `messaging.py` to properly disable/re-enable the send button during requests.
14	- Fix `agentic/tools/__init__.py` missing imports for `update_issue_*` and the missing comma in `__all__`.
15	- Fix `agentic/tools/updater.py` `update_dialog_style` to target `bubble_styles` instead of non-existent `dialog_style`.
16	- Fix `agentic/tools/creator.py` `create_issue` to set `Issue.name` (not `title`) and correct `series_id`.
17	- Fix `agentic/tools/creator.py` default `insertion_location` to an instance of `AfterLast`.
18	- Fix `agentic/tools/imaging.py` to initialize `style.image` dict before writing keys.
19	- Fix `agentic/tools/imaging.py` styled image ID format string in `create_styled_image_for_character_variant`.
20	- Fix `schema/styled_variant.py` `id` to return `image_id` and `name` to include the image identifier.
21	- Fix `storage/local.py` `list_uploads` to use reference upload paths, not image paths.
22	- Fix `storage/local.py` `find_reference_image` to remove undefined `relation` and use reference uploads.
23	- Fix `storage/filepath.py` StyleExample path templates and `IMAGE_PATH_TEMPLATES` keying.
24	- Fix `schema/reference_image.py` `primary_key`/`parent_key` semantics to support reliable storage and lookup.
25	- Fix `gui/issue.py` delete button to call `post_user_message(state, ...)` with state.
26	- Fix `gui/style.py` and `gui/scene.py` use of `style.id` to `style.style_id`.
27	- Fix `gui/style.py` to handle `style.image` being `None` before indexing.
28	- Fix `gui/panel.py` `aspect_ratio={aspect}` to pass a string, not a set.
29	- Fix `gui/panel.py` `panel_selector` selection building (`state.selection` vs `state`).
30	- Fix `gui/scene.py` panel image rendering path (`scene.path` and `image.py` placeholders).
31	- Fix `gui/reference_image.py` to use object-specific uploads rather than global `data/uploads`.
32	- Fix `gui/reference_image.py` missing f-string in `name = "Panel {parent.panel_number}"`.
33	- Fix `gui/style.py` `storage.read_object(...)(...)` call in `view_pick_style`.
34	- Fix `main.py` missing space when concatenating `MIDDLE_STYLING_CLASSES`.
35	- Make cover generator text elements configurable in `agentic/tools/imaging.py`.
36	- Add missing toolkits for character name update, variant creation from image, and variant deletion.
37	- Add scene reordering and panel reordering tools and UI support.
38	- Add issue name update tool and wire it into the issue toolkit.
39	
40	## Data Model Expansion
41	- Add `User`, `Organization`, `Workspace`, `Membership`, and role/permission models.
42	- Add `Project` or `Title` entity to group series, issues, assets, and collaborators.
43	- Add `Page` / `Spread` models for publishing layout, trim, bleed, and placement.
44	- Add `Asset` model for images with metadata (source, version, tags, ownership).
45	- Add scene setting metadata model (location, time, weather, mood, props, lighting).
46	- Add multi-angle reference set model for scenes and locations.
47	- Add `Comment` / `Annotation` model for collaboration feedback on any entity.
48	- Add `Version` / `Change` model for audit history and revert.
49	- Add `Task` / `Approval` model for editorial workflow (review, approve, publish).
50	- Define consistent ID generation rules across all models.
51	
52	## Storage & Scalability
53	- Replace local JSON file storage with a scalable database (Postgres or equivalent).
54	- Implement migrations for all schema changes and seeded data.
55	- Build a data migration tool from current local storage to the new database.
56	- Move images to object storage (S3/GCS/R2) with signed URLs and metadata.
57	- Add indexing strategy for fast search across series/issues/scenes/panels.
58	- Add pagination and batching for large lists in UI and API.
59	
60	## Backend/API Layer
61	- Introduce a service layer (CRUD + business logic) between UI and storage.
62	- Implement API endpoints for all entities with validation and error handling.
63	- Add WebSocket or realtime channels for collaboration updates.
64	- Add access-control middleware to enforce roles and workspace isolation.
65	- Add rate limiting and request tracing for AI and image endpoints.
66	
67	## AI & Agent System
68	- Add prompt templates for panels, scenes, issues, and series (currently missing).
69	- Implement panel image generation (prompt building + render + selection).
70	- Implement scene-to-panels storyboard generation using structured outputs.
71	- Add a single-shot storyboard/beatboard tool to generate a full panel set from a scene.
72	- Add AI critique and revision tools (beat notes, pacing, continuity).

3	def extract_format_keys(fmt) -> set[str]:
4	    from string import Formatter
5	    """
6	    Extracts the field names from a format string.
7	    Args:
8	        fmt (str): The format string to parse.
9	    Returns:
10	        set: A set of field names found in the format string.
11	    """
12	    return {field_name for _, field_name, _, _ in Formatter().parse(fmt) if field_name}
13	
14	if __name__ == "__main__":
15	    fmt = "{name} is {age} years old and lives in {city}."
16	    keys = extract_format_keys(fmt)
17	    print(f"Extracted keys: {keys}")  # Output: {'name', 'age', 'city'}
18	    
19	    fmt2 = "Hello, {user.name}! You have {user.notifications} new notifications."

1	# UX Ideas: Path-Based Views for Multi-Window Sessions
2	
3	## Problem
4	Today the app generates pages on the fly at the root path. Because the URL never changes, users cannot open multiple browser windows (or tabs) that point to different objects in the same session. We need stable, path-based routes so each window can deep-link to a specific view while sharing the same authenticated session.
5	
6	## Goals
7	- Each window has a distinct URL that encodes the current selection.
8	- URLs are deep-linkable and reload-safe.
9	- Session auth stays shared across windows (cookie-based).
10	- Back/forward works in a predictable way.
11	
12	## Design Alternatives
13	
14	### 1) Hierarchical Resource Routes (most direct)
15	Use explicit, human-readable paths for the current selection, with IDs embedded.
16	
17	Examples:
18	- `/series/:seriesId`
19	- `/series/:seriesId/issue/:issueId`
20	- `/series/:seriesId/issue/:issueId/scene/:sceneId`
21	- `/series/:seriesId/issue/:issueId/scene/:sceneId/panel/:panelId`
22	- `/styles/:styleId`, `/characters/:characterId`, `/publishers/:publisherId`
23	
24	Pros:
25	- Clear, shareable URLs and breadcrumbs.
26	- Works naturally with browser history.
27	- Easy to add “Open in new window” using the current URL.
28	
29	Cons:
30	- Requires consistent routing rules across all entities.
31	- Needs server fallback for client-side routes (serve app shell for all routes).
32	
33	### 2) Workspace + Selection Query (simple and flexible)
34	Keep a workspace path and encode the selection in the query string.
35	
36	Examples:
37	- `/workspace/:workspaceId?sel=series:123`
38	- `/workspace/:workspaceId?sel=issue:456&parent=series:123`
39	- `/workspace/:workspaceId?sel=panel:999&parent=scene:321`
40	
41	Pros:
42	- Minimal routing surface; the app always loads one route.
43	- Easy to extend without restructuring path hierarchy.
44	- Works well if selection types change frequently.
45	
46	Cons:
47	- URLs are less readable.
48	- Requires parsing to build breadcrumbs.
49	
50	### 3) View State IDs (server-stored view snapshots)
51	When a user navigates, create a short-lived view state on the server and use it in the URL.
52	
53	Examples:
54	- `/view/:viewId` where `viewId` maps to `{ selection, breadcrumbs, filters }`.
55	
56	Pros:
57	- Fully decouples UI state from URL structure.
58	- Can include complex UI state (filters, tabs, grid modes).
59	
60	Cons:
61	- Not transparent; URLs are opaque.
62	- Requires storage and TTL policies.
63	- Deep links expire unless persisted.
64	
65	### 4) Generic Resource Route + Tab Context
66	Use a consistent path shape for all resources and store the UI “panel” or mode as a query.
67	
68	Examples:
69	- `/r/series/:id?tab=issues`
70	- `/r/scene/:id?tab=panels`
71	- `/r/panel/:id?tab=images`
72	
73	Pros:
74	- Very small router surface area.
75	- Easy to add new entity types.
76	
77	Cons:
78	- Less semantic than hierarchical routes.
79	- Requires more client logic to resolve context.
80	
81	## Recommendation (if we want clarity + shareable links)
82	Start with **Alternative 1 (Hierarchical Resource Routes)** or **Alternative 4 (Generic Resource Route)**.
83	
84	- Alt 1 is best for readability and user mental model.
85	- Alt 4 is best for engineering speed and extensibility.
86	
87	Both approaches support multiple windows because each window has its own URL, and session auth can stay cookie-based. The critical change is to move selection state into the URL (path and/or query) instead of keeping it only in a single in-memory root view.
88	
89	## UX Notes
90	- Provide a visible “Open in new window” action near breadcrumbs or headers.
91	- Make breadcrumbs reflect the URL so sharing a link preserves context.
92	- On refresh, rehydrate selection from URL and load data accordingly.
93	
94	## Multi-Window Constraint: Single-Threaded Chat Context
95	Current chat context is single-threaded and global. This prevents parallel, scoped conversations per window, which is a core expectation for MDI (each window/tab should carry its own context and chat history).
96	
97	Implications:
98	- Opening two windows would mix or overwrite conversation context.
99	- Replies could target the wrong selection if both windows share one chat thread.
100	
101	Mitigation Ideas:
102	- Introduce `conversation_id` scoped to the URL (e.g., query param or route state).
103	- Bind chat history and AI context to `conversation_id` + selection path.
104	- Optionally allow “fork conversation” when opening a new window, so users can branch context intentionally.
105

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE AUDIT REPORT

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Comic Studio** is an AI-first collaborative comic book authoring and publishing platform. It's a Python/NiceGUI web application that allows creators to work with AI agents as collaborative peers, managing the full creative hierarchy from series definition through character design, story development, and panel/cover image generation.

**Key Philosophy**: Rather than traditional "generate" buttons with rigid templates, every interaction flows through natural conversation with context-aware AI agents that understand the current selection and available tools.

**Project Status**: Active working experiment in AI-first design (not production software). Core loop is functional and producing real output.

---

### 2. OVERALL ARCHITECTURE

**Tech Stack**:
- **Language**: Python 3.12+
- **UI Framework**: NiceGUI 2.19+ (Python web framework)
- **AI Orchestration**: OpenAI Agents SDK (not simple chat wrapper—full function-calling agents)
- **LLM**: GPT-5.2 (text generation and function calling)
- **Image Generation**: gpt-image-1.5 (generation, inpainting, outpainting)
- **Data Validation**: Pydantic 2.11+
- **Image Processing**: Pillow 11.2+
- **Storage**: Local JSON files (abstract storage interface)
- **Logging**: Loguru

**Architectural Approach**: AI-first with 14 specialized agents, each with:
- Custom persona (system instructions tailored to role)
- Filtered toolkit (only relevant tools available in context)
- Full object context (selection data, parent hierarchy, related assets as structured context)

**60+ Function Tools** covering:
- Story CRUD (series, issues, scenes, panels, dialogue, narration)
- Character management (create, variants, appearance/attire/behavior)
- Visual operations (panel/cover images, character reference sheets, style examples)
- Editing (inpainting, outpainting, region-based image editing)
- Style management (art/character/dialog styles, visual anchors)
- Navigation (context-aware selection changes)

**Entry Point**: `/Users/darrell/git/comics/.teaparty/jobs/job-20260407-181611--i-would-like-to-audit-the-code/worktree/main.py` — NiceGUI page with layout initialization, UI state management, and messaging loop.

---

### 3. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE & MAIN MODULES

```
worktree/
├── main.py                 # Application entry point, UI layout init
├── messaging.py            # Message handling, AI event streaming, UI updates
├── dateutils.py            # Date utility functions
├── test.py                 # Test file
├── pyproject.toml          # Project metadata & dependencies
├── README.md               # Comprehensive project documentation
├── BUGLIST.md              # Known bugs (6 documented)
├── TASKLIST.md             # Comprehensive task backlog (149+ items)
├── UX_ideas.md             # Path-based URL routing design options
├── LICENSE                 # All rights reserved
│
├── agentic/                # AI agent orchestration
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── toolkits.py         # Maps selection kind → available tools (14 toolkits)
│   ├── instructions.py     # Agent personas (12+ defined)
│   ├── style.py            # Agent styling/formatting
│   ├── constants.py        # Boilerplate instructions
│   │
│   └── tools/              # 60+ function-calling tools
│       ├── __init__.py     # Exports with MISSING COMMA BUG
│       ├── creator.py      # Create series, issues, scenes, panels, characters, variants, covers
│       ├── reader.py       # Read operations with BUG on line 98 (invalid any() usage)
│       ├── updater.py      # Update all entity fields
│       ├── deleter.py      # Delete operations
│       ├── imaging.py      # Image generation, inpainting, outpainting, style examples
│       ├── navigation.py   # Selection navigation with BUG (direct mutation on line 73)
│       ├── context.py      # Read context for agents
│       ├── formatting.py   # Prompt/output formatting
│       └── normalization.py # ID/name normalization
│
├── gui/                    # NiceGUI user interface
│   ├── __init__.py

│   │
│   ├── image_editor.py     # Image inpaint/outpaint UI
│   └── image_editor_choices.py # Image selection after generation
│
├── schema/                 # Pydantic data models (entity definitions)
│   ├── __init__.py         # Re-exports all models
│   ├── enums.py            # SelectedKind, Relation, CoverLocation, AspectRatio enums
│   ├── publisher.py        # Publisher
│   ├── series.py           # Series
│   ├── character.py        # CharacterModel

│
```
